taskDescription: Click on the key neuroanatomy terms related to the ventricular system and sensory pathways.
textField:
The *ventricular* system consists of two *lateral* ventricles, the *3rd* ventricle, and the *4th* ventricle. The lateral ventricles communicate with the 3rd ventricle via the *interventricular* foramen (of *Monro*). The 3rd ventricle connects to the 4th via the *cerebral* *aqueduct* (of *Sylvius*). *CSF* is produced by the *choroid* *plexus* and absorbed through *arachnoid* *granulations* into the superior *sagittal* sinus.

*Hydrocephalus* may be *obstructive* (non-communicating) or communicating. *Aqueductal* *stenosis* causes dilation of the lateral and 3rd ventricles with a normal 4th ventricle. CSF exits the 4th ventricle through the foramina of *Magendie* (median) and *Luschka* (lateral) into the *subarachnoid* space.

At the *optic* *chiasm*, *nasal* retinal fibres *decussate* while *temporal* fibres remain *ipsilateral*. A *pituitary* tumour compressing the chiasm causes *bitemporal* *hemianopia*. The visual pathway relays in the *lateral* *geniculate* body of the *thalamus* before projecting to the primary visual cortex in the *occipital* lobe via the *optic* radiation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review ventricular system and visual pathway terminology.
Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key neuroanatomy terms accurately.
